Bug / Task Description: NextRetry computes the exponential backoff one step too aggressively. The very first retry (attempt 1) already waits BackoffCoefficient times InitialInterval instead of waiting exactly InitialInterval, because the growth exponent applied to BackoffCoefficient is off by one. Every returned delay is therefore scaled by one extra factor of the coefficient before the MaxInterval ceiling is applied. Fix the calculation so attempt 1 yields InitialInterval, attempt 2 yields InitialInterval*BackoffCoefficient, attempt 3 yields InitialInterval*BackoffCoefficient^2, and so on, still clamping to MaxInterval and preserving the existing zero/invalid-config guards and the (duration, ok) return contract.
Commit Subject: fix(engine): correct exponential backoff exponent for NextRetry
